era: 1997-present. KEXP 90.3 FM. The Morning Show host since 2001. E=3 -- intimate, personal, vulnerable broadcasting. Music Heals campaign founder. KEXP's most recognized voice and public face.

location.formative: KEXP 90.3 FM. Capitol Hill. The new KEXP building at Seattle Center (opened 2016). The Morning Show studio.
location.career: KEXP Morning Show host (2001-present). Music Heals founder. Social media ambassador. KEXP's public face.

members.name: John Richards
members.role: Morning Show host, Music Heals founder
members.note: KEXP's most recognized voice. On air since 1997. Morning Show since 2001. Personal, conversational, vulnerable style. Music Heals campaign for mental health awareness. The human curator in the streaming era.

associated_musicians.name: Cheryl Waters
associated_musicians.note: The Midday Show. Local/indie focus. KEXP host trio: Waters (130), Richards (131), Mazzone (132). Consecutive on-air voices.

associated_musicians.name: Darek Mazzone
associated_musicians.note: Wo' Pop. Global/world music focus. Completes the KEXP host trio at degree 132.

associated_musicians.name: Bruce Pavitt
associated_musicians.note: Sub Pop co-founder. E=3 fanzine curation. Same curatorial instinct as Richards' on-air curation, different medium, different decade.

associated_musicians.name: Jonathan Poneman
associated_musicians.note: Sub Pop co-founder. Built label infrastructure. Richards operates radio infrastructure. Two forms of curatorial institution.

associated_musicians.name: Nasty Nes Rodriguez
associated_musicians.note: KCMU hip-hop DJ. Pioneered genre-crossing on Seattle radio. Richards continues the tradition at KEXP.

theory_nodes.concept: KEXP host trio (130-132) -- three consecutive on-air hosts documenting the station's personality infrastructure: Cheryl Waters (130, Midday, local), John Richards (131, Morning, personal), Darek Mazzone (132, Wo' Pop, global); three facets of one curatorial mission
examples: Waters (130): The Midday Show. Seattle-focused. Indie/alternative core. Warm, knowledgeable, steady.
Richards (131): The Morning Show. Personal, vulnerable, community-building. Music Heals. Public face.
Mazzone (132): Wo' Pop. Global music. Expanding KEXP beyond indie rock into world music.
Together: local + personal + global = KEXP's full mission.
theory_nodes.description: KEXP host trio. Three consecutive degrees, three on-air voices, one station, one mission.

ancestors: KCMU/KEXP institutional history (126-128). The station as cultural infrastructure. Now documenting the on-air voices.
Pavitt's curatorial ear (128) applied to daily broadcasting across three shows.
genealogy.relationship: Host trio at degrees 130-132. Track KEXP connections.

theory_nodes.concept: Music Heals -- mental health awareness as radio programming; Richards' signature initiative connecting music with emotional well-being; listener vulnerability as broadcast content; proved a music station can serve community health functions
examples: Listener stories of music as therapy -- grief, depression, anxiety, loss.
Stories read on air with permission. Radio as support group.
Grew from single-day event to year-round ethos. Ahead of its time.
theory_nodes.description: Music Heals. Radio as community health. Vulnerability as broadcast content.

ancestors: KEXP's listener-supported model enables non-commercial programming decisions.
The curatorial tradition: music as more than entertainment.
genealogy.relationship: Music Heals at degree 131. Track broadcast-as-community-health patterns.

theory_nodes.concept: The human curator vs. the algorithm -- Richards as the counterpoint to Spotify/Apple Music recommendation engines; personal, contextual, vulnerable, surprising; the parasocial relationship as value proposition; the radio DJ's relevance argument in the streaming era
examples: Algorithmic playlists: data-driven, impersonal, optimized for engagement.
Richards' Morning Show: taste-driven, personal, optimized for connection.
The parasocial relationship: listeners feel they know Richards. Algorithms have no personality.
Global reach through KEXP app: a local human voice distributed worldwide.
theory_nodes.description: Human curator vs. algorithm. The radio DJ's relevance in the streaming era.

ancestors: The fanzine-to-label pipeline (128): curation evolves through media.
genealogy.relationship: Human vs. algorithm at degree 131. Track how curation adapts to new media.

theory_nodes.concept: E=3 intimate broadcasting -- conversational, personal, vulnerable energy on air; the Morning Show as one-on-one conversation not performance; vulnerability requires quiet energy; curatorial intimacy consistent with Pavitt (128) and Waters (130)
examples: Richards speaks to the listener as an individual, not a crowd.
Music Heals embodies E=3: vulnerability shared carefully.
Contrast with commercial morning radio (E=5/E=6 performance energy).
E=3 consistency: Pavitt (128), Waters (130), Richards (131) all at E=3.
theory_nodes.description: E=3 intimate broadcasting. Vulnerability as energy. Curation as intimacy.

ancestors: E=3 calibrated at Pavitt (128) for curatorial energy.
KEXP's institutional character: intimacy, not spectacle.
genealogy.relationship: E=3 broadcasting at degree 131. Consistent with KEXP's on-air energy profile.

cross_references.relationship: KEXP HOST TRIO! Waters (Midday, 130) and Richards (Morning, 131) as consecutive KEXP on-air voices. Both E=3. Two shows, one station, one curatorial philosophy.
KEXP HOST TRIO COMPLETES! Mazzone (Wo' Pop, 132) adds the global dimension. Waters (local), Richards (personal), Mazzone (global). Three degrees, three facets.
CURATORIAL LINEAGE! Pavitt (E=3, fanzine/label curation) and Richards (E=3, on-air curation). Same energy, same city, same instinct. Different decades, same curatorial DNA.
INSTITUTIONAL CONTINUITY! Poneman (Sub Pop infrastructure) and Richards (KEXP infrastructure). Label executive and radio host: two forms of curatorial institution.
KCMU/KEXP LINEAGE! Nasty Nes (KCMU, 126, E=4) pioneered genre-crossing on Seattle radio. Richards (KEXP, 131, E=3) continues the tradition. Same station lineage, different eras.
